chapter_title.en: Chapter 30 – Office-bearers for the Sultan (ruling authority) and their recompense

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/63)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/63)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ أَعْمَالِهِمْ فَقَالَ لِي يَا أَبَا مُحَمَّدٍ لَا وَ لَا مَدَّةَ قَلَمٍ إِنَّ أَحَدَهُمْ لَا يُصِيبُ مِنْ دُنْيَاهُمْ شَيْئاً إِلَّا أَصَابُوا مِنْ دِينِهِ مِثْلَهُ أَوْ قَالَ حَتَّى يُصِيبُوا مِنْ دِينِهِ مِثْلَهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hisham Bin Salim, from Abu Baseer who said,

‘I asked Abu Ja’farasws about being their (Clan of Umayya’s) office bearers, so heasws said to me: ‘O Abu Muhammad! No, and not (even) for a circumflex (a mark on a letter like Fatah, Kisra etc.) of a pen. One of you would not attain anything from their world except he would lose from his Religion similar to it’, or said: ‘Until he loses from his Religion, similar to it’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘ Umayr from Hisham ibn Salim from abu Basir who has said the following: “I once asked abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about working for them (sultans). He (the Imam) said, ‘O abu Muhammad, no, not even for moving a pen. For whatever one finds from them of the worldly things, one’s religion is harmed proportionately or that he (the Imam) said that until his religion is harmed; - uncertainty is from ibn abu ‘Umayr.’”
